actors.name: Company Admin / Owner
actors.description: Sets up projects, defines budgets, manages client billing, and oversees overall job costing and profitability.

actors.name: Project Manager
actors.description: Plans work, assigns tasks to field workers, monitors timeline and budget vs actual, and communicates project status to clients.

actors.name: Field Worker
actors.description: Executes assigned tasks, logs daily hours against tasks, and records materials used on-site.

actors.name: Client
actors.description: Views project progress, receives and approves change orders, gets invoices, and reads LLM-generated status reports.

journeys.title: Create a new project with budget and timeline
journeys.goal: Set up a new construction or remodeling project so the team can start planning work and tracking costs.

steps.title: Enter project basics
steps.intent: The admin enters the project name, selects or creates the client, and fills in the site address so the project is properly identified and linked to the right customer.

steps.title: Define budget and schedule
steps.intent: The admin sets the project budget in USD and the planned start and end dates so that job costing and timeline tracking have a baseline to compare against.

steps.title: Activate the project
steps.intent: The admin sets the project status to active so it appears on the dashboard and the project manager can begin adding tasks.

journeys.outcome: A new project exists with a client, address, budget, timeline, and active status, ready for task planning and cost tracking.
businessRules: Budget must be a positive amount in USD.
Start date must be on or before the end date.
A project must be linked to a client before it can be activated.

journeys.title: Review dashboard for profitability and at-risk projects
journeys.goal: Get a quick snapshot of all active projects to see which are on budget, which are over, and which have overdue tasks.

steps.title: See active projects at a glance
steps.intent: The admin opens the dashboard to see all active projects with their budget vs actual cost summary so they can spot projects that are trending over budget.

steps.title: Identify overdue and upcoming tasks
steps.intent: The admin reviews the upcoming and overdue task list to understand where field work is falling behind and may need intervention.

steps.title: Drill into a concerning project
steps.intent: The admin clicks into a project that looks over budget or behind schedule to see the detailed task list, time logs, and material costs that explain the variance.

journeys.outcome: The admin has a clear picture of portfolio health and knows which projects need attention.
businessRules: Budget vs actual is calculated as (time log hours × worker rate) + material costs + approved change order amounts.
Only active projects appear on the dashboard.

journeys.title: Generate an invoice for a project
journeys.goal: Produce an accurate invoice based on logged time, materials, and approved change orders so the client can be billed.

steps.title: Select the project to bill
steps.intent: The admin chooses a project that has accumulated time logs, material usage, and approved change orders and opens the billing summary to review what will be invoiced.

steps.title: Review the billing summary
steps.intent: The admin reviews the consolidated cost breakdown — labor hours × rates, material costs, and approved change order amounts — to confirm everything is accurate before generating the invoice.

steps.title: Generate and send the invoice
steps.intent: The admin generates the invoice and sends it to the client via a shareable link or email so the client can review and pay.

journeys.outcome: An invoice is generated from actual job costs and delivered to the client for payment.
businessRules: Only approved change orders are included in invoice totals.
Invoice amounts are calculated as labor cost + material cost + approved change order amounts.
All figures are in USD; tax calculation is excluded.

journeys.title: Plan and assign tasks to field workers
journeys.goal: Break down the project into work tasks and assign them to the right field workers with due dates so the crew knows what to do and when.

steps.title: Open the project to plan work
steps.intent: The project manager opens the project detail view to see the current task list and timeline so they can identify what work still needs to be planned.

steps.title: Create work tasks with descriptions
steps.intent: The project manager creates individual work tasks with clear descriptions of what needs to be done so field workers understand the scope of each task.

steps.title: Assign workers and set due dates
steps.intent: The project manager assigns each task to a specific field worker and sets a due date so responsibility is clear and the timeline can be tracked.

steps.title: Review the task timeline
steps.intent: The project manager reviews the simplified task sequence timeline to make sure the ordering and due dates make sense relative to the project end date.

journeys.outcome: The project has a set of assigned tasks with due dates visible on the timeline, ready for field execution.
businessRules: A task must be assigned to a worker before it can be started.
Task due dates should fall within the project start and end dates.
The timeline is a simplified task sequence view, not a CPM scheduling engine.

journeys.title: Monitor project progress and budget health
journeys.goal: Check how the project is tracking against budget and timeline so the PM can take corrective action before problems escalate.

steps.title: Open the project detail
steps.intent: The project manager opens the project detail to see the current task statuses, consolidated cost summary, and timeline so they can assess overall progress.

steps.title: Review budget vs actual costs
steps.intent: The project manager compares the budgeted amount against actual labor and material costs to see if the project is trending over budget.

steps.title: Check for tasks at risk of delay
steps.intent: The project manager reviews the delay risk suggestions to identify tasks that are behind schedule or approaching their due date without progress so they can reassign or adjust.

steps.title: Reassign or adjust tasks
steps.intent: The project manager reassigns at-risk tasks to available workers or adjusts due dates to keep the project on track.

journeys.outcome: The project manager has identified budget or timeline risks and taken corrective action on specific tasks.
businessRules: Delay risk is determined by task status and proximity to due dates.
Actual cost = (time log hours × worker rate) + material costs + approved change order amounts.

journeys.title: Generate and share an AI status report with the client
journeys.goal: Produce a professional, plain-language status report from project data so the client stays informed without the PM spending time writing it manually.

steps.title: Generate the status report
steps.intent: The project manager clicks 'Generate Status Report' on the project detail page so the system compiles tasks, time logs, and material usage into a professional summary using the LLM proxy.

steps.title: Review the generated report
steps.intent: The project manager reads the generated report to make sure it accurately reflects project progress, costs, and any delay risks before sharing it with the client.

steps.title: Share the report with the client
steps.intent: The project manager sends the status report to the client via a shareable link or email so the client has a clear, professional view of where the project stands.

journeys.outcome: A professional status report is generated from real project data and shared with the client.
businessRules: The status report is generated from tasks, time logs, and material data — not manually written.
LLM calls go through the platform LLM proxy.

journeys.title: Create a change order and send it for client approval
journeys.goal: Document a scope change with its cost impact and get client approval before proceeding so the project budget and billing stay accurate.

steps.title: Start a change order
steps.intent: The project manager creates a change order on the project to document a scope change requested by or discussed with the client, including a description of the change.

steps.title: Specify the cost impact
steps.intent: The project manager enters the cost amount associated with the change so the client understands the financial impact before approving.

steps.title: Send to the client for approval
steps.intent: The project manager sends the change order to the client for in-app approval so there is a record of the client agreeing to the scope and cost change.

journeys.outcome: A change order is created with a cost amount and sent to the client for approval.
businessRules: Change orders use in-app approval status; e-signature is out of scope.
Only approved change orders are included in job costing and invoicing.
A change order must be linked to a project.

journeys.title: Log daily hours and materials on-site
journeys.goal: Record the time spent and materials used on assigned tasks so job costing stays accurate and the PM knows what was done.

steps.title: See today's assigned tasks
steps.intent: The field worker opens the app to see which tasks are assigned to them and what is due today so they know what to work on.

steps.title: Log hours worked on a task
steps.intent: After completing work on a task, the field worker logs the hours spent so that labor costs are captured for job costing.

steps.title: Record materials used
steps.intent: The field worker records the materials consumed on-site with their costs so that material costs are tracked against the project budget.

steps.title: Update task status
steps.intent: The field worker marks the task as in progress or completed so the project manager and dashboard reflect current progress.

journeys.outcome: The field worker's hours and materials are recorded against the task, and the task status reflects current progress.
businessRules: Time logs must be linked to a specific task and worker.
Material usage must be linked to a specific project.
Worker hourly cost rate comes from the platform user profile.

journeys.title: View project progress and status reports
journeys.goal: See how the project is progressing, read status reports, and understand what work has been done so the client feels informed and confident.

steps.title: Open the shared project view
steps.intent: The client opens a shareable link or portal to see the project overview, including current status and progress summary.

steps.title: Read the latest status report
steps.intent: The client reads the AI-generated status report to understand what work was completed, what is in progress, and any delay risks in plain language.

steps.title: Review costs to date
steps.intent: The client reviews the consolidated cost summary to see how actual spending compares to the agreed budget.

journeys.outcome: The client has a clear understanding of project progress, costs, and any risks without needing to call the PM.
businessRules: Clients access project information via shareable links or email; portal login uses platform auth when enabled.
Clients see the same status report the PM generated and reviewed.

journeys.title: Review and approve a change order
journeys.goal: Understand a proposed scope change and its cost, then approve or reject it so the project can proceed with the updated scope.

steps.title: Receive the change order notification
steps.intent: The client receives a change order via a shareable link or email and opens it to review the description and cost impact.

steps.title: Approve or reject the change
steps.intent: The client decides whether to approve or reject the change order based on the scope description and cost so the PM knows whether to proceed.

journeys.outcome: The change order is approved or rejected, and the project manager is notified of the decision.
businessRules: In-app approval status is sufficient; e-signature is out of scope.
Only approved change orders affect job costing and invoicing.

journeys.title: Review and pay an invoice
journeys.goal: Review the invoice details — labor, materials, and change orders — so the client understands what they are being billed for.

steps.title: Receive the invoice
steps.intent: The client receives an invoice via a shareable link or email and opens it to review the billing summary.

steps.title: Review the billing breakdown
steps.intent: The client reviews the itemized costs — labor hours and rates, materials, and approved change orders — to confirm the charges are accurate and expected.

journeys.outcome: The client has reviewed the invoice and understands the charges, ready to arrange payment.
businessRules: Invoices include labor cost, material cost, and approved change order amounts.
Payment gateway integration and collections are out of scope; the invoice is informational.

features.title: Project Lifecycle Management
features.description: Create and manage projects with name, client, address, status, budget, start/end dates, and client linkage.
features.rationale: Core foundation — every other capability depends on a project existing.

features.title: Work Task Creation and Assignment
features.description: Create work tasks with descriptions, assign them to field workers, set due dates, and track task status through completion.
features.rationale: Essential for field team coordination and timeline tracking.

features.title: Field Time Logging
features.description: Field workers log hours worked against specific tasks so labor costs feed into job costing.
features.rationale: Time logs are a primary input for job costing and budget vs actual comparison.

features.title: Material Usage Tracking
features.description: Record materials used on-site per project with associated costs for job costing and budget tracking.
features.rationale: Material costs are a major component of construction job costing.

features.title: Change Order Workflow
features.description: Create change orders with scope description and cost impact, send to clients for in-app approval, and track approval status.
features.rationale: Scope changes are common in construction; tracking them keeps billing and budgets accurate.

features.title: Invoice Generation and Billing Summary
features.description: Generate invoices from accumulated time logs, material costs, and approved change orders, and deliver to clients via shareable links or email.
features.rationale: Client billing is a core revenue function for construction companies.

features.title: Project Dashboard
features.description: Dashboard showing active projects, budget vs actual cost, and upcoming/overdue tasks for portfolio-level oversight.
features.rationale: Gives leadership and PMs a quick health check across all active projects.

features.title: Project Detail View
features.description: Project detail page with task list, simplified timeline, and consolidated cost summary showing labor, materials, and change orders.
features.rationale: The central hub where all project information converges for managers and clients.

features.title: AI-Powered Status Report
features.description: Generate a professional plain-language status report from tasks, time logs, and materials using the platform LLM proxy, and share it with clients.
features.rationale: Saves PM time and improves client communication with consistent, data-driven reports.

features.title: Delay Risk Identification
features.description: Identify and suggest tasks at risk of delay based on task status and due date proximity, surfaced on the dashboard and project detail.
features.rationale: Proactive delay alerts help PMs take corrective action before deadlines are missed.
